tools.name: recommend_agent_configuration
tools.description: Diagnose why a deployed AI agent may fail. Takes environmental entropy, predictability, stakes, context horizon, and commitment style, then returns receiver profile values (TI, SG, FT, UE, AR), platform parameters (temperature, top_p, strategy), regime prediction, reasoning, and warnings. Determinis

tools.name: interpret
tools.description: Detect ambiguity in user messages using the RPCS-1 Signature Ambiguity Framework. Returns AR level (AR0-AR5), confidence, candidate interpretations with scores, clarifying questions, and suggested next step. Use when a user says something vague, passive-aggressive, or underspecified.

tools.name: normalize
tools.description: Clean up text with ellipses, fragments, and run-on thoughts into coherent prose. Use when a user types stream-of-consciousness or fragmented input.

tools.name: rewrite
tools.description: Get rewrite instructions for adapting text to a specific style: technical, plain, socially_gentle, concise, detailed, or direct. Use when communication needs tone adjustment.

tools.name: calibrate_profile
tools.description: Build a ReceiverProfile (TI, SG, FT, UE, AR — continuous 0-100, never a category label) from five behavioral forced-choice answers. Call with NO answers to get the five questions to ask the user; call again with their answers (a/b/c per primitive) to get the profile. Store the returned profile JSON

tools.name: prepare_prompt
tools.description: The inbound half of the Translation Bridge loop. Takes the user’s raw message (possibly ambiguous, fragmented, or underspecified) plus their ReceiverProfile, and returns the recovered intent, a canonical translation to act on, ambiguity level, and — profile-aware — whether to clarify or commit. tools.name: render_reply
tools.description: The outbound half of the Translation Bridge loop. Takes your draft reply plus the user’s ReceiverProfile and returns deterministic rendering instructions (structure, warmth, explicitness, revision posture, ambiguity handling — each with a why-trace). Apply the instructions to your draft before answe

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "recommend_agent_configuration" appears informational but accepts parameters (environment) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "render_reply" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: The outbound half of the Translation Bridge loop. Takes your draft reply plus the user’s ReceiverProfile and returns deterministic rendering instructions (struc…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
